mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Poisson: Mean and Variance are the same (λ), like a twin. Normal: Bell curve is 'Perfectly Balanced' (μ) and 'Consistently Spread' (σ²).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: For Poisson: The mean E[X] = λ is derived from the sum of x * P(X=x). The variance Var(X) = λ is derived using E[X²] - (E[X])².

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: For Normal: The mean μ is the symmetry point of the bell curve. The derivation for μ and σ² involves solving the integrals for x*f(x) and (x-μ)²*f(x) over all real numbers.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A key relationship: The Poisson distribution with a large λ (e.g., λ > 20) can be approximated by a Normal distribution with μ = λ and σ² = λ.
